title: Context-cued recall — boost retrieval for same-mode and same-scope matches (encoding-specificity principle)
depends_on: U10 / future ticket — per-ticket/project scope must ship first (current_scope field on agent)
current_state: L2 + L3 retrieval treats every fact identically regardless of which mode or scope the user is currently in. A fact written in god mode about a side-project ranks the same as a fact written in root mode about Pi infrastructure when both match the query semantically. Pi already logs mode at L1 write time (raw_wiki.metadata.mode) and L2 metadata.session_id, so the data exists — it's just not used during retrieval.
structural_problem: Encoding-specificity (Tulving, 1973) is one of the most robust findings in memory research: recall is best when retrieval context matches encoding context. Without context-cued recall, Pi treats every fact as context-free even though context is the strongest disambiguator at small scale.
target_state: memory_read accepts optional current_mode and current_scope kwargs. _hybrid_search_l3 applies retrieval boosts: +0.15 to cosine score for same-mode match, +0.20 for same-scope match. Composes with salience (T-134) and decay (T-135). PiAgent passes self.mode + self.current_scope to all memory_read calls. Env-toggleable PI_CONTEXT_CUED_RECALL=on (default on after 1-week soak).
files_affected: tools/tools_memory.py — memory_read accepts current_mode + current_scope; _hybrid_search_l3 applies boosts in scoring loop
pi_agent.py — _prefetch_memory and any other memory_read call sites pass current context
design_notes: Boost values: same_mode_boost=0.15, same_scope_boost=0.20 (scope is stronger context signal than mode)
Boosts are ADDITIVE on cosine score (0-1 range) — never override semantic relevance for off-context-but-highly-relevant rows
Privacy invariant: god mode reads from god_memory.db only (existing namespace isolation); boosts compose within namespace
scope_match: row.scope == current_scope (exact). NULL scope (global) = no boost, no penalty
mode_match: row.metadata.mode == current_mode. NULL mode = no boost, no penalty (handles legacy rows pre-T-024)
Order of composition: cosine_score + entity_bonus + importance_nudge + same_mode_boost + same_scope_boost → final score
Wrap in try/except (Inv. 9) — on boost-computation failure, fall back to pre-boost score
success_criteria: Two L2 rows with equal cosine to query, different mode → same-mode row wins on retrieval order
Same setup with different scope → same-scope row wins (stronger boost than mode)
Off-context-but-highly-semantically-relevant row still surfaces (high cosine beats moderate boost)
god mode retrieval never returns public-namespace rows (existing test guard, double-check still holds)
risk_notes: Low risk — order-only change, no schema migration, no new infra. Risk: boost values miscalibrated → off-context relevant facts ranked too low. Mitigation: env flag instant revert; A/B logging shows which retrievals changed ordering under cued vs legacy.
migration_plan: 1. Add current_mode + current_scope optional kwargs to memory_read.
2. Modify _hybrid_search_l3 scoring loop to apply boosts when kwargs present.
3. Update all memory_read call sites in pi_agent.py + agent/* to pass self.mode + self.current_scope.
5. Log retrieval-ordering deltas via track_silent for analysis.
6. Flip default to on after soak shows no regressions.
progress: 2026-05-30: FOUNDATION shipped - l3_cache.mode column added (idempotent migration); memory_write records mode. Tests in test_memory_context_columns.py. REMAINING: _hybrid_search_l3 + _l3_fast_path same-mode boost behind PI_CONTEXT_CUED_RECALL; memory_read current_mode kwarg; pi_agent passes self.mode at write sites.
